macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Consumer Sentiment Hits 74-Year Low at 49.8
items.summary: The University of Michigan's final April reading shows consumer sentiment at its lowest point in the survey's 74-year history, with three of the four lowest readings occurring in the past nine months. Economists attribute the decline to wage stagnation amid persistent inflation, with real average hourly earnings expected to turn negative in May due to Middle East supply shocks.

items.title: Real Wages Face Headwinds as Inflation Persists Above 4%
items.summary: Average hourly earnings rose only 3.6% year-over-year while April inflation is expected around 4%, intensified by the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran and gas prices exceeding $4.55 per gallon. Chief economists warn that real average hourly earnings will likely be flat to negative in May, effectively representing a pay cut for workers.

items.title: Labor's Share of GDP Hits Historic Low
items.summary: Labor's share of GDP has reached its lowest level in Bureau of Labor Statistics history, continuing a two-decade downward trend. The muted wage growth in recent jobs reports reflects a widening gap between capital and labor, raising concerns about income inequality.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: $86.63M in Crypto Futures Liquidations, Mostly Shorts, as BTC Hits $81K
items.summary: Over the past 24 hours, cryptocurrency futures saw $86.63M in liquidations across the network, with $56.9M from shorts and $29.7M from longs; BTC liquidations totaled $16.88M while BTC price broke through $81,000, up 0.60%. ETH liquidations reached $12.63M.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Reaches $2.68T with 1.39% Gain; BTC at $80,767, ETH at $2,329
items.summary: Total crypto market capitalization hit $2.68 trillion, up 1.39% in 24 hours; Bitcoin rose 0.39% to $80,767 with 60% dominance, Ethereum up 0.50% to $2,328.66 holding 10.4% share.

items.title: Solana Breaks $90, Triggers $21M Short Liquidations Exceeding BTC's
items.summary: Solana surged past $90, causing over $21M in short liquidations—more than Bitcoin's $16M—backed by strong ETF inflows since January, with potential rallies to $110-$120.

items.title: Top Gainers: BPX Surges 1930%, GPM 1674%, NFLXX 793%; DeFi TVL Up 0.21%
items.summary: Black Phoenix (BPX) led with 1929.95% gain to $2.81, Gold Pump Meme (GPM) up 1674% to $0.011, Netflix tokenized stock (NFLXX) rose 793% to $895; DeFi TVL hit $85.53B up 0.21%, NFT sales down 54.73%.

items.title: Altcoins Surge: Uniswap +13.6%, Terra Luna Classic +12.5%, SUI +8.8%; ETH Whales Net Buy $42M
items.summary: Bitcoin held at $80,900 up 0.66%, Ethereum at $2,328 up 0.65% with $42M whale net buying; altcoins led by Uniswap to $4.14, SUI to $1.15 amid DeFi momentum and upcoming token unlocks for Avalanche, Aptos.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Announces 8th Generation TPU Chips with Dual Architecture
items.summary: Google released its 8th generation TPU chips with two specialized variants: TPU 8t for training and TPU 8i for inference. The company claims this architectural shift reduces frontier model training from months to weeks and can scale to a million chips in one logical cluster.

items.title: Canvas Learning Management System Hacked, Affecting Higher Education Institutions
items.summary: Canvas, a widely-used learning management system, was hacked on Friday, creating uncertainty for students and faculty at thousands of schools nationwide. The incident has raised concerns about data security in educational technology.
items.source: LiveNOW from FOX / Higher Ed Dive

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Clash of Clans New Update Reviewed: Sky Wagon, Logger Guardian, and Electro Fangs Introduced
items.summary: Judo Sloth Gaming provides an in-depth review of the latest Clash of Clans update, covering new equipment like Sky Wagon, Logger Guardian, and Electro Fangs, plus changes to the ranked system.

items.title: VALORANT Champions Tour Americas Match Point Post Show Features ENVY Eggster & P0ppin
items.summary: Post-show discussion on the VALORANT Champions Tour Americas league, where 12 teams compete for 3 spots at Masters London from April 10 to May 24.

items.title: Big New Mega Pokemon ex Revealed: Mega Chandelure and More
items.summary: New Pokemon TCG reveals include the highly anticipated Mega Chandelure ex, showcased in a video with details on upcoming sets like Chaos Rising and Pitch Black.

items.title: Best New Decks for Infinite Rank in Marvel Snap Guide Released
items.summary: A new YouTube guide breaks down how to play the top new decks to reach Infinite rank in Marvel Snap, focusing on current meta strategies.

items.title: Donovan Mitchell Build Dominates NBA 2K26 Random Rec Matches
items.summary: A powerful new Donovan Mitchell-inspired slasher guard build is taking over NBA 2K26's random rec mode, featuring legend badges like Posterizer and Ankle Assassin.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Intel Signs Preliminary Deal to Produce Chips for Apple
items.summary: Intel has reportedly signed a preliminary agreement to manufacture chips for Apple, marking a significant shift in Apple's chip sourcing strategy and potentially reshaping the semiconductor supply chain.

items.title: Sony Partners with TSMC to Build New Image Sensor Facility
items.summary: Sony is teaming up with chip manufacturing giant TSMC to establish a new facility for producing image sensors, expanding their production capabilities.

items.title: Porsche Discontinues Performance E-Bike Division
items.summary: German automaker Porsche is shutting down its performance e-bike division to refocus on its core business operations.

items.title: FCC Allows Critical Updates for Banned Drones and Routers Until 2029
items.summary: The Federal Communications Commission has issued a notice permitting software and firmware updates for banned drones and routers through January 2029.

items.title: Boox Announces Tappy Wireless Page-Turning Remote
items.summary: Boox has unveiled Tappy, a wireless page-turning remote that works with e-readers and other Bluetooth-enabled devices.

items.title: Instructure Hackers Claim Data Theft from Nearly 9,000 Schools
items.summary: Hackers claim to have stolen data from approximately 9,000 schools through Instructure's Canvas platform and have locked students out, demanding negotiation by May 12.
